runningDialog.title: Instance already running
runningDialog.desc: This instance is already running. You can go to the window or restart it after closing.

initGuide.title: Instance not initialized
initGuide.desc: This is a blank instance. Only the directory exists and instance data has not been generated yet.
initGuide.tip: Please start this instance once. You can bind an account after initialization is complete.

restartStrategy.title: Restart strategy
restartStrategy.desc: Choose how instances are restarted. Each option affects scope and stability.

safe.title: Safe restart (recommended)
safe.principle: Send SIGTERM to the target instance processes so the app can save and exit, then relaunch that instance (force kill if needed).
safe.pros: Closer to a normal exit, less likely to trigger crash prompts, safer for data.
safe.cons: May take longer while waiting for a graceful shutdown.

force.title: Force restart
force.principle: Match processes by --user-data-dir and terminate them forcibly (SIGKILL / taskkill /F), then relaunch the target instance.
force.pros: Can precisely restart a single instance.
force.cons: May trigger “unexpected quit” prompts and risk losing unsaved data.

stop.title: Force close instance
stop.message: A termination signal (SIGTERM) will be sent to force close this instance, which may cause data loss. Continue?

delete.title: Delete Instance
delete.message: Delete instance {{name}}? This will remove the config and delete the instance folder.

runHint.title: Verification notes
runHint.line1: Detect now sends a minimal chat request by simulating Antigravity with a local gateway and the official Language Server protocol.
runHint.line2: This consumes a small amount of tokens (usually one short dialogue). Actual usage depends on model and account policy.
runHint.line3: This operation does not modify wakeup task settings. It only checks account availability and writes detection records.
runHint.line4: If a 403 is returned, account verification or an appeal submission may be required. The result will include the URL and quick actions.

dialogs.riskWarning:
⚠️ Risk warning
Due to recent Google risk-control tightening, using the wakeup feature may increase the chance of triggering account risk controls (including but not limited to additional verification or temporary limits).
Please use it with caution based on your account status and evaluate the risk yourself.

Continue?

form.triggerModeHint:
1. Scheduled: trigger at set times.
2. Crontab: define with an expression.
3. Quota reset: trigger once when quota is full.
4. After startup: trigger once after app start, with optional delay.

codebuddy.title: CodeBuddy Account

flowNotice.title: CodeBuddy Account Info (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Switching accounts requires reading CodeBuddy local auth storage and calling system credential services for decryption/re-encryption. Data is processed locally only.
flowNotice.permission: Permission scope: read CodeBuddy auth database (state.vscdb), call system credential capabilities (macOS Keychain / Windows DPAPI / Linux Secret Service) for decrypt/write-back.
flowNotice.network: Network scope: OAuth login and token refresh require network requests to codebuddy.ai; quota queries call billing APIs. No local keys or credentials are uploaded.

codebuddy.oauthDesc: Click the button below to open the CodeBuddy authorization page in your browser.
codebuddy.oauthDescWebview: Click the button below to open the CodeBuddy authorization page in built-in WebView.

oauth.item2: Resource-package quota data will refresh automatically after authorization.
oauth.item3: Account cards show quota amount, progress, and refresh/expiry time by resource package.

githubCopilot.title: GitHub Copilot Account

unsupported.desc: GitHub Copilot multi-instance is available only on macOS.

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your GitHub Copilot accounts

oauth.desc: Click the button below and complete GitHub Copilot authorization in your browser.

token.desc: Paste your GitHub Copilot access token or exported JSON data.

import.localDesc: Import GitHub Copilot account data from a JSON file.

githubCopilot.injectSuccess: Token injected to VS Code successfully. VS Code is launching.
githubCopilot.injectRequireClose: Please close VS Code before injecting.

flowNotice.title: GitHub Copilot account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Switching accounts requires reading VS Code local auth storage and using the system credential service for decrypt/write-back. Data is processed locally only.
flowNotice.inject: One-click switching: This tool reads local VS Code encryption material (DPAPI/Keychain/Secret Service), decrypts the auth session in state.vscdb, replaces the GitHub Copilot token, and re-encrypts it — all locally on your machine.
flowNotice.reason: Permission scope: read VS Code auth database (state.vscdb) and call system credential capability (Windows DPAPI / macOS Keychain / Linux Secret Service) for decrypt/write-back.
flowNotice.storage: Data scope: only GitHub auth-session related entries are read/updated; all processes run locally.

summary.label: Overview
summary.title: Account Snapshot
summary.desc: See Antigravity and Codex at a glance

panels.emptyDesc: Add an account to see details

fileCorrupted.title: File Read Failed
fileCorrupted.description: File {{fileName}} is corrupted and cannot be parsed.
fileCorrupted.helpText: Please open the folder to repair manually or delete the file, then restart the application.

autoSwitch.title: Auto Switch
autoSwitch.thresholdDesc: Auto switch when monitored model groups hit this percentage threshold
autoSwitch.hint: Automatically switch to the account with the highest quota when monitored model groups hit the threshold.

quotaAlert.title: Quota Alert
quotaAlert.thresholdDesc: Trigger alert when any model quota falls below this percentage
quotaAlert.hint: When any current-account model quota drops below the threshold, send a native notification and show a quick-switch action in the app.

modal.title: Quota Alert
modal.desc: The current account quota has reached the alert threshold. Please handle it soon.

unsupported.desc: Windsurf multi-instance is available only on macOS.

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your Windsurf accounts

oauth.desc: Click the button below and complete Windsurf authorization in your browser.

token.desc: Paste your Windsurf access token or exported JSON data.

import.localDesc: Import Windsurf account data from a JSON file.

flowNotice.title: Windsurf account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Switching accounts requires reading Windsurf local auth storage and, with client-compatible rules, using system credential/encryption capabilities for decrypt/write-back. Data is processed locally only.
flowNotice.inject: One-click switching: This tool reads local Windsurf encryption material (DPAPI/Keychain/Secret Service), processes the auth session in state.vscdb, writes back the Windsurf token, and keeps everything local on your machine.
flowNotice.reason: Permission scope: read Windsurf auth database (state.vscdb) and use system encryption capabilities (Windows DPAPI / macOS Keychain / Linux Secret Service) to decrypt and write back auth-session data.
flowNotice.storage: Data scope: only Windsurf auth-session related entries are read/updated; all processes run locally.

password.desc: Sign in with your Windsurf account email and password to automatically fetch API key and account info.

flowNotice.title: Kiro account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Account switching reads and writes local Kiro auth files and instance settings on this device; OAuth login and quota refresh call Kiro official APIs. For Enterprise/IdC accounts, refresh also calls AWS IAM Identity Center OIDC APIs.
flowNotice.reason: Processing paths include ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json, instance profile.json, and the kiro.kiroAgent entry in state.vscdb.
flowNotice.storage: Processed data includes Kiro session and quota information; requests to Kiro official APIs send required auth fields (such as code, refreshToken, accessToken, and profileArn). Enterprise/IdC refresh additionally sends required OIDC fields (such as client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token).

unsupported.descPlatform: Kiro multi-instance is available only on macOS, Windows and Linux.

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your Kiro accounts

oauth.desc: Click the button below and complete Kiro authorization in your browser.

token.desc: Paste your Kiro access token or exported JSON data.

import.localDesc: Import Kiro account data from local Kiro client or a JSON file.

unsupported.descPlatform: Cursor multi-instance is supported on macOS, Windows, and Linux only.

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your Cursor accounts

oauth.desc: Click the button below and complete Cursor authorization in your browser.

token.desc: Paste your Cursor Access Token (JWT) or exported JSON data.
token.formatRequired: Single token: paste JWT directly; batch import: use JSON array format

import.localDesc: Import account data from local Cursor client or a JSON file.

flowNotice.title: Cursor account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Manage Cursor accounts by importing from local Cursor installation, pasting a JWT token, or signing in via OAuth.
flowNotice.reason: Permission scope: read and write Cursor local auth storage (state.vscdb) for account import and token injection (account switching).
flowNotice.storage: Network scope: when refreshing quota, the JWT is sent to cursor.com official APIs to fetch usage, user info, and subscription data. No data is sent to any third-party server.

instances.unsupportedDescPlatform: Gemini Cli multi-instance is supported on macOS, Windows, and Linux only.

instances.workingDirDesc: Will switch to this directory first upon startup

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your Gemini Cli accounts

oauth.desc: Click the button below and complete Gemini Cli OAuth authorization in your browser.

token.desc: Paste your Gemini Cli Access Token (JWT) or exported JSON data.
token.formatRequired: Single token: paste JWT directly; batch import: use JSON array format

import.localDesc: Import Gemini Cli account data from local Gemini Cli config directory (~/.gemini) or a JSON file.

projectConfig.title: Set GCP Project
projectConfig.empty: No available project found. Ensure this account has access to GCP projects.
projectConfig.hint: After saving, this project is preferred for quota requests and injected as GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT in launch commands.
projectConfig.cleared: Switched back to automatic project resolution
projectConfig.savedWithRefreshError: Project saved, but quota refresh failed: {{error}}

flowNotice.title: Gemini Cli account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Manage Gemini Cli accounts by importing from local Gemini Cli config, pasting a JWT token, or signing in via OAuth.
flowNotice.reason: Permission scope: read and write oauth_creds.json, google_accounts.json, and settings.json under default ~/.gemini or instance path (GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini) for account import and local credential injection (account switching).
flowNotice.storage: Network scope: OAuth and refresh call official Google/Gemini APIs (such as oauth2.googleapis.com and googleapis.com). Only required auth/quota fields are sent; local account files are not uploaded to third-party servers.

breakout.serveHint: Press Space to launch the ball
breakout.closeConfirmDesc: Closing will end this run and progress will not be kept.

cautions.0: If you see abnormal statuses (for example 403/refresh failed), refresh first, then decide whether to remove and re-login.

outcomes.0: Supports OAuth, import, export, and bulk operations.
outcomes.1: Supports tag filtering, sorting, and grouped display.

steps.0: Click "Add Account" to complete authorization or import.
steps.1: Use "Refresh All" to sync one full round of quota status first.
steps.2: Sort by quota/reset time and choose the account you want to use now.

antigravity.summary: Manage the Antigravity account lifecycle: add, refresh quota, switch, group, and tag.
antigravity.title: Antigravity Account

cautions.0: Dashboard is for the big picture. For fine-grained operations, go to the specific platform page.

outcomes.0: See multi-platform "current account / recommended account / quota overview" on one page.
outcomes.1: Use quick switch buttons to reduce page-hopping cost.

steps.0: First check whether each platform card has account and quota data.
steps.1: If status is abnormal, click refresh or enter the corresponding platform page to handle it.
steps.2: If you need to change platform display order, open "Platform Layout" management.

dashboard.summary: For global overview: quickly check account status on each platform, use recommended switching, and jump to feature pages.
dashboard.title: Dashboard

cautions.0: Before importing JSON from untrusted sources, validate it in a test environment first.
cautions.1: Before deletion and bulk operations, confirm filters first to avoid accidental deletion.
cautions.2: Before sharing logs, redact sensitive data. Do not paste full token, cookie, email, etc.

outcomes.0: Import accounts in bulk for fast environment migration.
outcomes.1: Export JSON for backup or cross-device migration.
outcomes.2: Locate issues via error hints and file repair guidance.
outcomes.3: Quickly locate runtime issues through app.log* in Data Directory/logs.

steps.0: Before bulk operations, export current data once as a snapshot.
steps.1: On list pages, switch privacy view via "Show/Hide Email".
steps.2: When file corruption prompts appear, follow the dialog and open the directory to repair.
steps.3: For troubleshooting, go to "Settings -> Data Directory -> Open", then enter logs folder.
steps.4: Check the latest app.log or app.log.* first (date-rotated log files).
steps.5: When submitting feedback, include: occurrence time, platform, reproduction steps, key error logs (20 lines before/after).

dataPrivacy.summary: Covers daily maintenance: JSON import/export, masked email display, exception handling and recovery flow.
dataPrivacy.title: Import/Export, Privacy & Troubleshooting

cautions.0: Before deleting a fingerprint, check bound account count first to avoid affecting production accounts by mistake.

outcomes.0: Generate/capture new fingerprints and maintain metadata.
outcomes.1: View accounts bound to a fingerprint and add/remove bindings.

steps.1: Open details and confirm fingerprint info is correct.
steps.2: In binding management, bind accounts to target fingerprints.

fingerprints.summary: Manage fingerprint templates and account bindings. Supports generation, capture, import, and binding maintenance.
fingerprints.title: Device Fingerprints

cautions.0: Before copying from source instance, close the source first to avoid data inconsistency.
cautions.1: You cannot bind an account before a blank instance is initialized. This is expected behavior.

outcomes.1: Run multiple accounts in parallel to reduce repeated login switching.
outcomes.2: Validate new config in a test instance first, then return to main instance.

steps.0: When creating a new instance, prefer "Copy Source Instance" for faster usability.
steps.1: If you choose "Blank Instance", start once for initialization, then bind account.
steps.2: After binding account, manage lifecycle via "Start / Locate Window / Stop".

instances.summary: Used for account isolation and parallel running. Each instance has independent directory and state to avoid cross-contamination.
instances.title: Multi-Instance (Key)

cautions.0: Account notes describe local read/write and permission scope. Read first before operating.
cautions.1: Some platforms or capabilities are system-limited. Follow on-page prompts.

keywords.0: Codex / GitHub Copilot / Windsurf / Kiro platforms

outcomes.1: GitHub Copilot/Windsurf/Kiro: support injection into VS Code chain.
outcomes.2: Each platform supports independent filtering, grouping, import, and export.

steps.0: Complete OAuth or Token import first.
steps.1: Confirm plan/quota and reset time are visible in account list.
steps.2: Execute "Switch/Inject" and verify in client that it takes effect.

providers.summary: The four platform pages share the same structure: account overview + multi-instance, with OAuth, Token/JSON import and switching.
providers.title: Codex / GitHub Copilot / Windsurf / Kiro Platforms

cautions.0: For first-time usage, operate one platform only. Finish one full flow before bulk import.
cautions.1: If a platform startup path is missing, fill path in settings before continuing.

outcomes.0: First check global status on Dashboard, then operate on specific platform pages.
outcomes.1: Complete one full closed loop on one platform first, then scale to others.
outcomes.2: Understand that "Account" and "multi-instance" are two different workflows.

steps.0: Open "Dashboard" and confirm the platforms you need are visible.
steps.1: Go to target platform page (for example Codex / GitHub Copilot), then add 1 account first.
steps.2: Use "Switch/Inject" button to verify account switching takes effect.
steps.3: Then go to "Multi-Instance" and create 1 instance to verify isolation and parallel running.

quickStart.summary: Follow the first-run sequence: "Check status -> Add account -> Switch account -> Create multi-instance" to avoid getting lost.
quickStart.title: Quick Start (5 minutes)

manual.searchPlaceholder: Search feature, scenario, or keyword (for example: multi-instance, inject, wakeup, fingerprint)

cautions.0: When path detection fails, manually selecting executable path is the safest option.
cautions.1: Too-low thresholds trigger frequent alerts. Start around 20%.

outcomes.0: Configure auto-refresh and quota alerts across platforms in one place.
outcomes.1: Set startup paths for each client and fix "missing path" issues.
outcomes.2: Adjust window behavior, language, and theme to fit your habits.

steps.0: In "General" tab, finish baseline language/theme/path setup first.
steps.1: Then adjust auto-refresh and alert threshold per platform.
steps.2: After network service port changes, restart as prompted to apply.

settings.summary: Centralized management for language, theme, auto-refresh, alert threshold, app paths, and network service settings.
settings.title: Settings & System Capabilities

manual.subtitle: Built-in instructions organized by task scenarios: know "why to use" first, then follow steps to "use immediately".
manual.title: User Manual

cautions.0: For first-time setup, select only a small number of accounts. Expand after stability is confirmed.
cautions.1: If verification result is abnormal, check errors and verification links in details first.

outcomes.0: Create recurring tasks and record historical execution results.
outcomes.1: Use verification page for batch checks and failure analysis.

steps.0: Create a new task in "Wakeup Tasks" and set model and trigger mode.
steps.1: Run manually once first to ensure task parameters are valid.
steps.2: On "Verification" page, run batch inspection and historical review.

wakeup.summary: Used for scheduled wakeup tasks and batch verification, helping continuous tracking of account availability and status.
wakeup.title: Wakeup Tasks & Verification

flowNotice.title: Qoder account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Supports official CLI device login authorization, local import, JSON import, account switching injection, multi-instance binding, and quota overview. Browser authorization does not require Qoder to stay running; injection writes back using Qoder client's actual on-disk rules.
flowNotice.permission: Permission scope: reads Qoder local auth storage, user info, and machine identity caches such as machine_token.json and cache/id for official authorization, local import, account switching, and session injection. All data is processed locally on your machine.
flowNotice.network: Network scope: OAuth authorization opens Qoder's official device login page in your browser and polls Qoder official OpenAPI to finish authorization. Quota queries call Qoder APIs for usage data. No local keys, credentials, or raw storage contents are uploaded.

oauth.hint: Click the button below to complete Qoder authorization in your browser. This uses the official CLI device login flow and does not require Qoder to stay running.

usageOverview.title: Usage Overview
usageOverview.upgradeHint: Upgrade your plan to increase monthly quota

comingSoon.title: Qoder Account Integration In Progress
comingSoon.desc: This release supports Qoder multi-instance, platform management, and tray entry. Account login and quota panels will arrive in a later release.

unsupported.descPlatform: Qoder multi-instance is available only on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

import.localDesc: Read the current sign-in state from the local Qoder client, including user info, plan, and credits usage.

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your Zed accounts, or import them from JSON or the current local sign-in state.

import.jsonDesc: Import a Zed JSON file exported by this tool.
import.localDesc: Import account data from the current local Zed sign-in state or an exported JSON file.

flowNotice.title: Zed Account Integration Notes (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Supports official OAuth sign-in, JSON import, importing the current local sign-in state, and applying a saved account back to the official Zed client using the client's real persistence rules. This page only shows status fields that the desktop client can read directly.
flowNotice.reason: Permissions: opens the official same-origin Zed sign-in in your browser; reads the current local Zed credentials for import; writes back to the same system credential slot when applying an account; and can start or restart Zed when needed.
flowNotice.storage: Data handling: only imported or authorized account records, tags, and exported content are stored locally. Local Keychain or credential contents are not uploaded, browser web sessions are not scanned, and unrelated system credentials are not modified.

page.subtitle: Supports official same-origin login, subscription and usage queries, real login overwrite, and Zed restart.
page.searchPlaceholder: Search GitHub login, plan, or tags

runtime.title: Current Zed Session
runtime.loadFailed: Failed to load Zed runtime status

oauth.pendingDesc: Complete the browser authorization, then come back here to finish importing the account.
oauth.importOnlySuccess: Authorization succeeded. The account has been imported. To make the official Zed use it, click "Apply and Restart".

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your Trae accounts, or import from local Trae data or a JSON file.

oauth.desc: Click the button below and complete Trae OAuth authorization in your browser.

import.localDesc: Import the current signed-in account, plan information, and cached quota data from the local Trae configuration directory.

quota.statusExhaustedPro: Status: Usage exhausted, upgrade or enable on-demand usage

flowNotice.title: Trae account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Supports official OAuth sign-in, local import, JSON import, and local injection-based account switching. Account switching writes back using Trae client's actual on-disk rules.
flowNotice.permission: Permission scope: read and write login-related fields in the local Trae storage.json for account import, account switching injection, and plan display. All data is processed locally on your machine.
flowNotice.network: Network scope: OAuth sign-in, token refresh, and plan queries call official Trae APIs. Local account files and raw storage contents are not uploaded to third-party services.

codebuddyCn.title: CodeBuddy CN Account
codebuddyCn.oauthDesc: Click the button below to open the CodeBuddy CN authorization page in your browser.
codebuddyCn.tokenDesc: Paste your CodeBuddy CN access token:

flowNotice.title: CodeBuddy CN Account Info (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Switching accounts requires reading CodeBuddy CN local auth storage and calling system credential services for decryption/re-encryption. Data is processed locally only.
flowNotice.permission: Permission scope: read CodeBuddy CN auth database (state.vscdb), call system credential capabilities (macOS Keychain / Windows DPAPI / Linux Secret Service) for decrypt/write-back.
flowNotice.network: Network scope: OAuth login and token refresh require network requests to codebuddy.cn and copilot.tencent.com; resource-package quota refresh calls billing APIs. No local keys or credentials are uploaded.

import.localDesc: Import account data from local CodeBuddy CN client or a JSON file.

unsupported.descPlatform: CodeBuddy CN multi-instance is supported on macOS, Windows, and Linux only.

checkin.title: Daily Check-in
checkin.failure.network: Network error, please try again later

workbuddy.title: WorkBuddy Account

flowNotice.title: WorkBuddy Account Info (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Switching accounts requires reading WorkBuddy local auth storage and calling system credential services for decryption/re-encryption. Data is processed locally only.
flowNotice.permission: Permission scope: read WorkBuddy auth database (state.vscdb), call system credential capabilities (macOS Keychain / Windows DPAPI / Linux Secret Service) for decrypt/write-back.
flowNotice.network: Network scope: OAuth login and token refresh require network requests to WorkBuddy; quota queries call billing APIs. No local keys or credentials are uploaded.

workbuddy.oauthDesc: Click the button below to open the WorkBuddy authorization page in your browser.
workbuddy.oauthDescWebview: Click the button below to open the WorkBuddy authorization page in built-in WebView.

oauth.title: IDE sign-in only
oauth.item1: Add the account after completing OAuth in your browser, then use it for IDE switching.
oauth.item2: Resource-package quota data will refresh automatically after authorization.
oauth.item3: Account cards show quota amount, progress, and refresh/expiry time by resource package.

workbuddy.oauthWebviewProgressHint: A horizontal step bar is shown at the top of the WebView to locate stuck stages.

oauthFlow.bind: Poll auth result and bind account

steps.bind: 3 Poll auth result and bind account

status.loginConfirm: Please complete sign-in and authorization on this page.

workbuddy.jsonDesc: Select a JSON file containing WorkBuddy accounts:

import.localDesc: Import account data from local WorkBuddy client or a JSON file.

workbuddy.oauthUrlInputPlaceholder: You can enter an authorization URL manually
workbuddy.oauthInvalidUrl: Invalid authorization URL. Please enter a full URL.
workbuddy.oauthGotoWebview: Navigate opened WebView to input URL
workbuddy.oauthGotoWebviewSuccess: Requested WebView to navigate to the input URL.
workbuddy.oauthWebviewInlineUrlPlaceholder: Enter a URL to navigate within this WebView
workbuddy.oauthSuccessClosePrompt:
Account added successfully. Close this WebView now?
Choose "Close WebView" to close now, or "Later" to keep it open.
workbuddy.oauthSuccessCloseLaterStatus: ✅ Account added. This WebView stays open.

unsupported.descPlatform: WorkBuddy multi-instance is supported on macOS, Windows, and Linux only.

mfaVault.pageDesc: Securely store and manage your MFA backup recovery codes, passcode sets, and other sensitive authentication data. MFA records are stored locally only. Deleting records, clearing cache, or replacing/losing a device may cause MFA loss. Keep backups and import from backup when needed.
mfaVault.confirmDeleteMsg: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the credential record for [{{accountName}}]?
mfaVault.importErrorMsg: Import failed. Please check the file format (must be a valid JSON array containing secret; accountName is optional).
mfaVault.otpauthInputHint: Supports otpauth://totp/... and Base32 Secret. Both formats can be queried and saved to the list.
mfaVault.pasteQrImageHint: You can also paste a QR image directly into the input box.
mfaVault.uploadQrImageTitle: Select a QR image and recognize it automatically
mfaVault.invalidOtpAuthInput: Please enter a valid otpauth://totp/... URI or Base32 Secret.
mfaVault.qrDecodeFailed: QR recognition failed. Please try a clearer image.
mfaVault.qrNotOtpAuth: Content was recognized, but it is not a valid otpauth://totp/... URI or Base32 Secret.

twoFactorAuth.pageDescNew: Securely store and manage your 2FA secrets and other sensitive authentication data. 2FA data is stored locally only. Deleting records, clearing cache, or replacing/losing your device may cause 2FA loss, so please keep your own backup.
twoFactorAuth.inputRemarkPlaceholder: [Optional] Set a remark name for this secret
twoFactorAuth.confirmDeleteSavedFallback: Are you sure you want to permanently delete this secret?
twoFactorAuth.confirmDeleteHistoryFallback: Are you sure you want to delete this query history?
twoFactorAuth.confirmClearAllMsg: Are you sure you want to clear all recent query history?
twoFactorAuth.importErrorMsg: Import failed. Please ensure the file format is a valid exported JSON array.
